purpose: Define the next wave of product initiatives after MVP completion, Sprint 1 demo-readiness closeout, and the first CI baseline.

required_preconditions: Minimal GitHub Actions CI baseline is green
The next feature branch should be cut from main after baseline promotion

working_mode.default_rule: One feature at a time for implementation, while allowing independent features from the same initiative to be planned in parallel.
working_mode.why_not_v4_shape: The v4 list was optimized for FE/BE/INT decoupled MVP construction. Growth work should stay legible as product capabilities first, with implementation slices nested under each initiative rather than becoming the top-level roadmap.

feature_status_meaning.in_progress: A branch or PR is actively executing this feature.
feature_status_meaning.completed: The implementation scope is landed, but passes may still require verification evidence.

passes_rules.meaning: passes=true means the feature has evidence appropriate to its type, not just that code exists.
default_expectation: Representative workflow or browser verification for user-facing flows
Progress-log or runbook evidence when a feature changes cross-surface behavior
passes_rules.note: Implementation work may reach completed before passes becomes true.

initiatives.title: Scholar & Reviewer Foundation
initiatives.goal: Move the current M4-lite profile foundation toward a reusable scholar directory and reviewer sourcing layer that can support the next wave of workflows.
initiatives.why_now: The simplified PRD treats M4 as the foundation for review, grants, prizes, schools, and future industry matching. The MVP already proved the base profile model; the next highest-leverage move is to strengthen search, reviewer enablement, and identity signals.

initiatives.title: Schools & Training Applications
initiatives.goal: Introduce a first real school/program application flow by reusing the shared application and review backbone already proven by conferences and grants.
initiatives.why_now: The simplified PRD ranks M8 as a P1 operational module. It is a better growth candidate than breadth-only surfaces because it reuses existing backbone capabilities while adding a new program type with clear user value.

initiatives.title: Workspace Usability & Copy Unification
initiatives.goal: Make applicant, reviewer, and organizer shells more consistent, predictable, and mobile-safe before the platform grows another layer of workflows.
initiatives.why_now: Sprint 1 removed the largest demo blockers, but the backlog still calls out status semantics, empty states, and secondary-page mobile polish as next-tier usability work.

initiatives.title: Jobs & Referee Workflow
initiatives.goal: Add the first concrete M3-facing employment application slice without turning the platform into a generic workflow engine.
initiatives.why_now: The simplified PRD still positions M3 as an important P1 module, but it brings email/referee/file-handling complexity. It should follow M4-next and one more reused application type, not preempt them.

features.title: Scholar directory search, filters, and pagination
features.description: Extend the current real-data scholar directory so public and logged-in users can search by name/institution and filter by research tags, MSC, country, or career-stage-adjacent signals.
features.why_now: This is the clearest direct follow-up to the current real-data directory. It turns the directory from a proof-of-data surface into a reusable discovery surface.
evidence_expectation: Backend/provider tests for query and paging contract
Browser smoke on /scholars using real data

features.title: Reviewer access request and enablement flow
features.description: Replace reviewer access being a purely manual admin action with an explicit request, review, and activation path.
features.why_now: The current reviewer role still relies on seeding or direct admin intervention. That is acceptable for demo, but not a good foundation for more review-driven product growth.
evidence_expectation: Representative UI/browser verification for request and approval path
Progress-log evidence showing how reviewer enablement now works

features.title: Reviewer candidate picker with expertise and conflict signals
features.description: Improve organizer/admin reviewer assignment by surfacing expertise match, reviewer-source eligibility, and clearer conflict notes instead of a thin candidate list.
features.why_now: The simplified PRD emphasizes M4 as the expert source. This feature gets you the product value of expert matching without committing to a large opaque ranking engine.
evidence_expectation: Backend/provider tests for candidate sourcing and conflict data

features.title: Academic identity and ORCID surface tightening
features.description: Strengthen profile identity surfaces by tightening ORCID link presentation, academic metadata consistency, and scholar-facing profile completeness signals.
features.why_now: The simplified PRD treats ORCID and academic identity as trust multipliers. MVP intentionally stopped at link-level support; this is the lightweight next step before OAuth or auto-sync.
evidence_expectation: Documentation of identity field expectations if contracts change

features.title: Scholar-to-reviewer integration acceptance
features.description: Run and record a full acceptance slice covering public scholar discovery, reviewer request/approval, and organizer assignment using the strengthened M4 surfaces.
features.why_now: M4-next only matters if the same foundation can be experienced across public discovery and internal review sourcing, not as disconnected admin tools.
evidence_expectation: Representative smoke or integration test coverage

features.title: School list and detail public surfaces
features.description: Add a real schools/training discovery surface with program metadata, application state, and support visibility.
features.why_now: The simplified PRD positions schools as the next program type after conferences and grants. Public list/detail is the smallest credible entry point.

features.title: School application flow on shared backbone
features.description: Implement the first school application flow by reusing application, file, status, and result-release patterns proven in M2-lite and M7-lite.
features.why_now: This validates whether the current backbone can support a third program type without becoming a generic system too early.
evidence_expectation: Real-flow verification for submit and released result states

features.title: Organizer participant selection and support allocation
features.description: Give organizers a minimal workspace for selecting school participants, allocating financial support, and releasing results.
features.why_now: A school application without an organizer selection path does not produce a real program workflow; this is the minimal completion of M8-lite.
evidence_expectation: Progress or runbook evidence for operator path

features.title: School application end-to-end acceptance
features.description: Verify the end-to-end school flow from public discovery to applicant submission to organizer selection and result release.
features.why_now: Each new program type should earn its place through a credible integrated story, not by accumulating isolated screens.

features.title: Dashboard and workspace status-copy unification
features.description: Systematically unify applicant, reviewer, and organizer status language, next-step labels, and empty-state semantics.
features.why_now: Sprint 1 fixed obvious mismatches such as active-count semantics, but copy drift still exists across shells and secondary pages.
evidence_expectation: Browser spot checks on affected shells

features.title: Secondary-page mobile and narrow-screen polish
features.description: Harden key secondary pages and workspace detail surfaces for mobile and narrow-screen layouts.
features.why_now: This is not a core product blocker, but it is the cleanest polish layer to land before another round of larger workflow expansion.
evidence_expectation: Regression tests where layout behavior is encoded
Targeted screenshots or notes in handoff

features.title: Workspace browser acceptance refresh
features.description: Re-run browser-level acceptance for applicant, reviewer, and organizer shells after copy and responsive polish land.
features.why_now: Workspace polish is easy to over-claim from unit tests alone; this initiative should close with explicit browser evidence.

features.title: Public job listings and detail pages
features.description: Add the first real public jobs surface with list/detail pages aligned to the Asiamath portal and opportunity model.
features.why_now: The simplified PRD keeps M3 important, but jobs should only enter after the team proves a third reusable application type or chooses to prioritize employment over schools.
evidence_expectation: Contract evidence for job opportunity model

features.title: Job application workflow
features.description: Implement a first employment application flow with job-specific materials while still reusing the shared backbone.
features.why_now: This is the first point where the platform begins to test M3 proper. It should wait until the team chooses it over M8-lite, not compete with that initiative in the same sprint.

features.title: Referee request and confidential letter upload
features.description: Add the first confidential referee management slice for employment applications, including request, reminder, and upload handling.
features.why_now: This is one of the biggest complexity multipliers in M3, so it should be explicit and delayed until after the team confirms that jobs are the next major product lane.
evidence_expectation: Workflow evidence for upload and visibility boundaries

features.title: Jobs and referee flow acceptance
features.description: Run an end-to-end acceptance slice for jobs, application submission, referee material handling, and organizer/admin visibility.
features.why_now: If the platform enters M3, it should do so with one complete and testable story rather than half-adopting the module.
